summary: Backend-only testing of super_admin welcome/password/logo fixes. All 24 previously existing pytest tests still pass. Added 9 new tests (test_owner_email_match_iter305.py) specifically covering: (a) send-welcome-to-owner picks admin whose email matches tenant.owner_email even when multiple admins exist in same tenant, (b) update_tenant with owner_password updates ONLY that admin (not any other admin like a general manager sharing the admin role), (c) fallback to oldest admin when no email match, (d) WA logo file is physically 56x56 / ~5KB (5005 bytes) vs email logo 240x240, (e) vault roundtrip for Arabic/special chars/emoji/spaces/empty/None. Also verified live endpoints: super-admin login (owner@maestroegp.com/owner123/271018) works; GET /api/super-admin/delivery-channels/status returns proper email/whatsapp/sms ready structure; POST /send-welcome-to-owner on missing tenant returns 404. Total: 33/33 pytest passed.

minor.issue: Review request stated POST but actual route is GET (routes/super_admin_routes.py:2737). Endpoint works correctly via GET.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is very large (>17000 lines) — continue extracting route modules like super_admin_routes.py for maintainability.
Multiple DeprecationWarning: @app.on_event('startup'|'shutdown') should migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers.

update_tenant (line ~806): the fallback to 'oldest admin by created_at' when no owner_email match is defensive but could still miss the real owner if seed data lacks created_at — consider requiring owner_email match strictly and returning 400 otherwise.
encrypt_plain_password(None) behavior should be documented / made explicit (currently accepted without raising).
seed_data_creation: None. All new tests use unittest.mock to patch db — no writes to real MongoDB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter305 verified the tenant.owner_email matching logic for both send-welcome-to-owner and update_tenant(owner_password). WA logo confirmed 56x56 at /app/backend/static/branding/maestro-logo-wa.png (5005 bytes). Both bug scenarios reported by user (random password sent + oversized WA logo) are now covered by regression tests. All 33 password/welcome/branding pytests pass. Note: GET (not POST) for /delivery-channels/status.
